title: Australia Sets New Rules for AI and Protects Creative Work
subtitle: The government announces a new AI office and strict copyright protections to stop the unauthorized use of Australian content.

summary.en: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has announced the creation of a new AI office to manage the growth of artificial intelligence in Australia. The government aims to protect local artists, writers, and journalists from having their work used by AI companies without permission. New, strict rules will also apply to large energy-intensive datacentres to ensure they do not harm housing or electricity costs. While tech companies have expressed a desire for clear rules, some experts worry about the power of global giants. This move follows recent international legal tensions involving AI firms and intellectual property.
summary.zh: 總理艾班尼斯宣布成立一個新的人工智慧辦公室，以管理澳洲人工智慧的發展。政府旨在保護在地藝術家、作家和記者，防止其作品在未經許可的情況下被人工智慧公司使用。針對耗能巨大的大型資料中心，政府也將實施嚴格的新規範，以確保其不會對住宅用地或電費造成負面影響。儘管科技公司已表達對明確規則的渴望，但部分專家仍擔憂全球科技巨擘的權力。此舉是在近期涉及人工智慧公司與智慧財產權的國際法律緊張局勢後所採取。

paragraphs.en: The Australian government has taken a major step toward controlling the influence of artificial intelligence. Prime Minister Anthony Albanese announced this week that Australia will establish a new office for AI. This office will help the country manage the rapid growth of technology and ensure that AI development follows clear, national standards. The announcement comes at a time when the government is facing pressure to address public concerns about how AI affects jobs, energy use, and the rights of creative workers.
paragraphs.zh: 澳洲政府已採取重大舉措，旨在控管人工智慧的影響力。總理艾班尼斯 (Anthony Albanese) 本週宣布，澳洲將成立一個專責人工智慧的新辦公室。該辦公室將協助國家管理科技的快速發展，並確保人工智慧的開發遵循明確的國家標準。此項宣布正值政府面臨壓力之際，必須解決大眾對於人工智慧如何影響就業、能源消耗以及創作者權益的擔憂。

paragraphs.en: During a speech at the University of Sydney, the Prime Minister made it clear that Australian creative work is not for sale to tech companies. According to The Guardian, Albanese stated that writers, musicians, artists, and journalists must keep control of their work. He warned that if AI companies use this content to train their models without permission or payment, it would be considered "theft." The government has ruled out allowing AI firms to use Australian data for free, rejecting proposals that would have made it easier for these companies to avoid paying creators.
paragraphs.zh: 在雪梨大學發表演說時，總理明確表示澳洲的創意作品絕非科技公司的囊中物。根據《衛報》(The Guardian) 報導，艾班尼斯指出，作家、音樂家、藝術家和記者必須保有對其作品的掌控權。他警告，若人工智慧公司未經許可或支付費用便使用這些內容來訓練模型，將被視為「竊盜」行為。政府已排除允許人工智慧公司免費使用澳洲數據的可能性，並拒絕了那些會讓這些公司更容易規避支付創作者報酬的提案。

paragraphs.en: This decision has been welcomed by groups representing the creative industry. Annabelle Herd, the CEO of the Australian Recording Industry Association (Aria), expressed relief at the Prime Minister’s strong stance. She noted that her organization is ready to sign licensing deals—agreements that allow companies to use creative work in exchange for payment—to ensure artists are fairly compensated. She encouraged AI companies to stop waiting and start talking to creators to reach fair agreements.
paragraphs.zh: 這項決定受到代表創意產業團體的歡迎。澳洲唱片業協會 (Aria) 執行長安娜貝爾·赫德 (Annabelle Herd) 對總理的強硬立場表示欣慰。她指出，她的組織已準備好簽署授權協議——即允許公司使用創意作品以換取報酬的合約——以確保藝術家獲得公平的補償。她鼓勵人工智慧公司停止觀望，並開始與創作者對話，以達成公平的協議。

paragraphs.en: Beyond copyright, the government is also introducing strict new rules for datacentres. These are large, energy-hungry buildings that store the data needed to run AI systems. The Guardian reports that the government plans to regulate where these facilities can be built. The goal is to make sure they do not compete with housing for land or drive up electricity prices for regular families. Operators will also be required to pay for the water infrastructure needed to keep these massive systems cool.
paragraphs.zh: 除了著作權之外，政府也針對資料中心引入了嚴格的新規範。這些是耗電量巨大的建築物，用以儲存運作人工智慧系統所需的數據。據《衛報》報導，政府計畫規範這些設施的興建地點。目標是確保它們不會與住宅用地競爭，或導致一般家庭的電費上漲。營運商也將被要求支付維持這些龐大系統冷卻所需的基礎設施費用。

paragraphs.en: Global tech companies have responded to the news with a mix of caution and cooperation. Jeff Bleich, a lawyer for the AI company Anthropic, said the firm respects the process the government is creating. He noted that the company takes its responsibilities seriously and wants to follow the rules set by Australian authorities. Previously, Anthropic had suggested that a lack of clear rules in Australia made it difficult for them to invest in the country. Similarly, Jane Livesey, the president of Microsoft Australia, said that people will only trust and use AI if they believe the technology is safe and well-governed.
paragraphs.zh: 全球科技公司對此消息的反應兼具謹慎與合作態度。人工智慧公司 Anthropic 的律師傑夫·布萊奇 (Jeff Bleich) 表示，該公司尊重政府正在建立的程序。他指出，公司認真看待自身責任，並希望遵守澳洲當局制定的規則。此前，Anthropic 曾表示澳洲缺乏明確規則，使其難以在該國進行投資。同樣地，微軟澳洲總裁珍·萊夫西 (Jane Livesey) 表示，唯有大眾相信這項技術安全且受到良好治理，才會信任並使用人工智慧。

paragraphs.en: However, some analysts suggest that the government faces a difficult task. While the Prime Minister’s plan is seen as a positive step, tech giants like Google, Microsoft, and OpenAI are often more powerful than individual national governments. As noted by The Guardian, these companies can set their own terms and prices, which may limit how much control Australia can truly have over global AI development. There is also the question of how the government will handle public fears about job losses, as AI is expected to change many parts of modern life in ways that are hard to stop with laws alone.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，一些分析師認為政府面臨艱鉅的任務。儘管總理的計畫被視為正面的一步，但像 Google、微軟和 OpenAI 等科技巨擘的權力往往大於個別國家政府。正如《衛報》所指出的，這些公司可以設定自己的條款和價格，這可能會限制澳洲對全球人工智慧發展的實際掌控力。此外，政府將如何處理大眾對於失業的恐懼也是個問題，因為人工智慧預計將以難以僅靠法律阻擋的方式，改變現代生活的許多層面。

paragraphs.en: This new focus on regulation follows a period of growing tension in the tech world. Just days ago, Apple took legal action against OpenAI, claiming that a former employee stole sensitive trade secrets. As these legal battles continue, the Australian government is trying to ensure that its own laws are strong enough to protect its citizens and culture. The government plans to bring new legislation to parliament in early 2027, following further discussions with state leaders next month.
paragraphs.zh: 這項對監管的新關注，源於科技界日益緊張的時期。就在幾天前，蘋果 (Apple) 對 OpenAI 採取法律行動，聲稱一名離職員工竊取了敏感的商業機密。隨著這些法律戰持續進行，澳洲政府正試圖確保其國內法律足以保護公民與文化。政府計畫在與各州領導人下個月進行進一步討論後，於 2027 年初將新法案提交國會。

paragraphs.en: Ultimately, the Prime Minister wants Australia to be more than just a place to store data for overseas products. He hopes that by setting these standards, the country can encourage local innovation while ensuring that the benefits of AI are shared fairly. Whether these rules will be enough to balance the power of global tech companies remains a significant challenge for the future.
paragraphs.zh: 歸根究柢，總理希望澳洲不僅僅是儲存海外產品數據的地方。他期望透過制定這些標準，在鼓勵在地創新的同時，確保人工智慧帶來的利益能被公平共享。這些規則是否足以平衡全球科技公司的權力，仍是未來的一大挑戰。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To decide that something is not possible or will not be allowed.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於口語，這裡指政府明確表示不會允許某事發生。文中：The government has ruled out allowing AI firms to use Australian data for free, rejecting proposals that would have made it easier for these companies to avoid paying creators.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To cause a price or level to increase quickly.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指數據中心對電力需求過大，導致電價上漲。文中：The goal is to make sure they do not compete with housing for land or drive up electricity prices for regular families.

keyTerms.definitionEn: The conditions or rules that are part of an agreement or deal.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「術語」，這裡指合約中的條件。文中：As noted by The Guardian, these companies can set their own terms and prices, which may limit how much control Australia can truly have over global AI development.

sources.name: The Guardian Technology
sources.title: ‘Not up for grabs’: Albanese establishes AI office and vows to protect Australian creatives from copyright ‘theft’

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: Albanese’s AI plan is admirable – but will face tech giants more powerful than most national governments

previousArticles.title: Apple Takes Legal Action Against OpenAI Over Stolen Secrets
previousArticles.summaryEn: Apple has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI regarding the theft of sensitive trade secrets. The company claims a former engineer used a software bug to access private files after leaving his job. This legal action follows earlier accusations that OpenAI hired former Apple staff to gain an unfair advantage in hardware development. Apple is now seeking court orders to stop OpenAI from using this stolen information. The case highlights the growing competition between tech giants as they race to build new AI-powered devices.

quiz.question: According to the article, what specific requirement will be placed on operators of datacentres?

options.A: They must limit the total amount of data stored to save electricity.
options.B: They are required to pay for the water infrastructure used for cooling.
options.C: They must relocate all existing datacentres away from residential housing.
options.D: They must provide free internet access to families living near the facilities.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第四段明確指出，政府將要求資料中心營運商必須支付冷卻這些大型系統所需的基礎水利設施費用。選項 A 文中未提及限制儲存量；選項 C 提到政府會規範建設地點，但並未要求搬遷現有設施；選項 D 關於免費網路的說法與文章內容無關。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the relationship between global tech companies and the Australian government?

options.A: Tech companies are completely refusing to comply with any new Australian regulations.
options.B: The government is confident that its new laws will easily override the power of global tech giants.
options.C: There is a tension between the government's desire for regulation and the significant influence of powerful tech corporations.
options.D: Tech companies are currently moving all their operations out of Australia due to the new AI office.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第六段分析，雖然政府試圖透過立法規範 AI，但文中提到這些跨國科技巨頭通常比個別國家政府更有權勢，且能制定自己的條款與價格，這顯示出政府在監管過程中面臨巨大挑戰。選項 A 與文中提到公司表示尊重與合作的態度不符；選項 B 與第六段提到的「困難任務」矛盾；選項 D 文中並未提及公司撤離澳洲。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the word 'hungry' imply when describing datacentres as 'energy-hungry buildings'?

options.A: The buildings require a very large amount of electricity to function.
options.B: The buildings are designed to be environmentally friendly and efficient.
options.C: The buildings are causing a shortage of food for the local population.
options.D: The buildings are constantly being expanded to hold more data.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在此上下文中，「energy-hungry」是比喻用法，意指這些資料中心運作時需要消耗極大量的電力資源，就像飢餓的人需要大量食物一樣。選項 B 與原意相反；選項 C 是對「hungry」的字面錯誤解讀；選項 D 雖然資料中心可能會擴建，但這並非「energy-hungry」一詞在文中所要表達的核心意義。

quiz.question: Which of the following best summarizes the main message of the article?

options.A: The Australian government is primarily focused on banning AI technology to protect creative workers.
options.B: Australia is taking proactive steps to regulate AI development and protect national interests despite the challenges posed by global tech giants.
options.C: Global tech companies have successfully convinced the Australian government to drop all copyright regulations.
options.D: The primary goal of the new AI office is to help Australian tech companies compete with OpenAI and Google.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。全文重點在於澳洲政府透過成立 AI 辦公室、規範資料中心及保護創作者權益等手段，試圖在 AI 快速發展的浪潮中建立國家標準，儘管面臨跨國科技巨頭的強大影響力，這仍是其核心政策目標。選項 A 錯誤，政府是「管理」而非「禁止」；選項 C 與事實相反；選項 D 文中並未將「協助本地公司競爭」列為主要目的，而是強調監管與公平。
